All right, welcome back friends. In this lesson, we are going to go over the anatomy of an empowered high value customer. And I don't mean that in a Grade 9 biology kind of way. I mean that more as the states, the emotional states, the commitments, the feelings that a high value customer will have prior to making that purchase. Cool. All right, with that clear, I do want to start with talking about the anatomy of a qualified non-buyer. And the reason why is that converting qualified non-buyers is the biggest opportunity in your business. It's a term that doesn't get thrown around too often, qualified non-buyers. So let's unpack that for a second. So what we mean by qualified non-buyer are people who should have bought based on where they're at and what your offer can do for them, but ultimately didn't buy for whatever reason and that whatever reason is usually lumped into the general rule that they just weren't coached or ascended properly towards that final transformation. So converting this segment of qualified non-buyers is the biggest opportunity. And to do so, we need to get real. We need to immediately eliminate words like tire kickers or deadbeats or other shaming terms that marketers way too casually throw around as if they've never experienced it themselves. Because in reality, it isn't any of these. It's actually just avoidance of a decision that's rooted in fear and certainty or mistrust. And unless you've personally never hesitated or felt resistance around a big decision before, well, we're acting out of integrity, but more importantly and more costly, missing an amazing opportunity. So the coach who helps transform someone from fear to courage will always win the client. And to do so, we need to get really, really good about engaging with qualified non-buyers. And remember the observer effect, if you see or treat or engage with them as a deadbeat or a tire kicker, chances are within your experience, they'll practically become one. But if you observe them more realistically and more reality based as a courageous pre-customer who's confronting some deep shit right now, instead you'll help them through it and become a better leader, a better coach, and a more successful coach in the process. And here's the thing. If you can't coach them through pre-enrollment resistance ethically, and if it's truly in their best interest, then they'll subconsciously and rightfully doubt your ability to coach them in your program once they come up against something uncomfortable. Resistance is going to be inherent in any transformation, whether that transformation is them joining your program from pre-customer to customer or in the transformation within your program where they actually go from their current state to a brand new state. That resistance is going to show up. And if you shut down and get derogatory and defensive around any degree of resistance instead of being able to see it for what it is, normalize it and coach them through it, then of course they're not going to become a customer because you're sending every signal that they're not safe in being a customer, that they actually won't get the results because there's no room for people who feel any ounce of doubt or resistance or fear within a transformative process. Cool. All that said, we need to have the discussion of low to mid-ticket versus mid to high ticket because the selling process is a little bit different when it comes to coaching the conversion. So for a low to mid-ticket sale to happen, your pre-customer really only needs to know and feel that they have a problem, also known as problem awareness, that you have a clear solution, also known as solution awareness, and that they trust your product to deliver and they trust themselves enough to yield the results. That is most aware. As for mid to high ticket, resonance becomes the secret X factor. So does this person resonate with me? It's language you probably use a lot in your own day-to-day interactions. If someone has amazing material but doesn't resonate with you, well, you might want to buy, but you won't be able to will yourself to do it. While someone with resonance and not a lot of information about their product can probably DM you with a PayPal link and you'll be like, yes, let's do it. So a big part of what we're doing here is unpacking where resonance actually comes from and doing what we can within our ACEs, within our automated conversion ecosystems to strategically engineer it across the marketing experience. Cool. So this is also no trust on steroids. So a prospect won't sit there and be like, I know you, I like you, I trust you, let's go. They'll literally be like, this resonates, I'm in, or let's talk. So knowing how to engineer this resonance is everything in your marketing. That's a big topic and a big focus of what we'll be doing with the playbooks later on. So the new economy. So web 2.0 was all about attention and engagement and engagement. Pages wanted to get all the likes they could. Coaches wanted their content to be viewed 800,000 times so that they could feel special and that they're making the impact they want. And there's nothing wrong with that. But attention and engagement are currencies that are starting to get devalued very fast. They're extremely scarce. And let's talk about this. So currency was attention and engagement and those have been devalued, diluted and dispersed. It's essentially a hyperinflation economy and they've all crashed. The only thing that matters now in 2021 and beyond is relevance, resonance and responsiveness. And responsiveness is literally what is needed for someone to respond to your offer. It's their ability to actually zero in and focus in on a single opportunity at a time, bring their attention in, narrow it down and actually make a decision that is empowered and in their best interest. Cool. So that's all that matters now. Relevance, resonance, something clicking on a deeper level and responsiveness, the ability to overcome overwhelm, focus on a decision and act courageously. So when we talk about coaching the conversion as the process of empowering the transformation between point A and point buyer, what we're really talking about is cultivating and amplifying relevance, resonance and responsiveness. And what is direct response if it's not systematically and ethically empowering our prospects ability to respond, otherwise known as making a decision. So the anatomy of an empowered high value customer, five things they need to feel seen, heard and understood. They need to feel resonance with the marketer, with the brand, with the diagnosis around their problem, with the prognosis of what's possible. And of course, the prescription later on of what to do about it. They need to feel clear on the real problem and the real solution and what must be done in a way that is relevant. And they need to feel connected to their own power, their ability, their self belief and their courage. And finally, to make that decision that responsiveness, they need to feel committed and accountable to their new reality. So that's really it. If we can knock down those dominoes, those five major conversion precursors, those five attributes of an empowered high value customer, then we're going to get an empowered successful repeat customer period every time. But these are all fairly big topics. So we're going to chunk it down and give every stage of our marketing, every automated conversion ecosystem the responsibilities of coaching those elements and not moving a prospect to another stage of the process until we're confident that those precursors have been met. So this is how we're going to do it across the three aces inside of automated intimacy. So the first ace, the enchanting first encounter, essentially their first interaction with our brand as soon as they get on our list. So what we're going to be coaching in this stage is relevance with what they think they want and need and what you're all about, what your content is about, what you're teaching and what you're training and what your coaching is all about. It needs to feel relevant to what they think they want or need. We're also going to be coaching resonance with you, your content, your ethos, your diagnosis and your progress. And we're going to start coaching responsibility. So not responsibility like a parent talking to their 13 year old about being more responsible, but their ability to actually respond, to engage in a two way dialogue, to have a shared responsibility and investment in the relationship between them and you as the coach, course, creator or service provider. So it's less one sided and there's a built in responsiveness right from the beginning. Cool. In ACE2, the Ascension Accelerator, we're going to be coaching momentum towards a point of no return or tipping point where there's more resistance and backtracking to their old reality than there is in naturally moving forward to the opportunity you're about to present to them. We're going to be coaching magnetism. This is where they make the decision that if it's going to be anybody, it's going to be you. Remember, there is only so much attention they can invest. So in this ACE, we are making sure that there is magnetism in relation to you, magnetism to you and in relation to others. And you've earned that attention. You've earned the focus on the enrollment conversation later on. And we're also going to be coaching mastery. So mastery and the method that you teach to solve the real problem. They need to feel at this stage that you, your service, your course is the right method, that you have that mastery to get them to where they need to go. Cool. And then finally, ACE3 engineering the enrollment. So this is all about the final sale. And what we're coaching here is achievability that they can get that result with your help. And to do that, they need to feel clear and confident in that offer and the process. They need to be coached into accountability for results once they join the program, but also for a decision. The more decisions we get, the more yeses we're going to get. So that's just kind of the law of sales. And unless we're actually actively coaching accountability for a decision, then our prospects are going to suffer from that inability to make the decisions that they need to make and that we need them to make in order to really make good on that journey between pre-customer and customer. And finally, actualization. So this is that point of no return, that second part of it, where it goes from ephemeral, something they may do to inevitable, something they absolutely must do and will be doing and already see and sense and feel themselves doing. So a lot of experienced salespeople will literally feel this shift happen on a call, where it actually gets actualized to not an abstract idea of something they may do or may become to a sense that they're already here. They're already embodying that post purchase state where they're in the process of the transformation that your coaching or your program would have them go under. Cool. So this is how it all works together. This is the automated conversion ecosystem experience. So we have our three main aces in our marketing and our sales and all of these essentially coach these states, these experiences, these virtues that over time help you hit these five milestones of an empowered high value buyer. So this is the journey from pre-customer to customer, from earning potential to earned reality. And as you can see, intimacy and income go up as we move across these aces and knock down more of these milestones. So this is really the core of everything to come. We're going to be building out these aces. We're going to be implementing these playbooks and in doing so, we're going to be coaching all these attributes that we just spoke about. We're going to be achieving these conversion precursors or these milestones. And in the process, we're going to be creating more empowered high value buyers, which is really the goal of all of this. All right. So the next lesson, we're going to talk about three tools for coaching the conversion. We're going to talk about touch point theory. We're going to talk about the idea of earned enrollment to keep everything in balance between us and our prospects. And we're going to talk about what we call conversion context that we use with automated intimacy. So take a breather, grab a sip of water, do a little stretches, and I'll see you in the next video.
